summary: Customer CUST-004 (Dana Whitfield) submitted vague web_form feedback stating billing is "weird" and the app "sometimes does the thing wrong." The feedback was classified as a billing_complaint with negative sentiment and medium urgency, but flagged as ambiguous with low classifier confidence (0.4). No specific charge, amount, or bug behavior was described, so no billing dispute can be verified from the text alone. The feedback also hints at a possible app bug that cannot be substantiated. Relevant billing SOP and policies were retrieved, along with the customer record; however, the underlying issue itself remains too vague to act on definitively without clarification.
customer_context: Retrieved record for CUST-004 (Dana Whitfield), standard tier, 14 months tenure. One prior resolved billing ticket (TCK-1290) requesting an invoice with a corrected company address. One active order ORD-8654, Standard monthly plan, $29.00, dated 2026-07-01.

suggested_actions.action: Reply to the customer requesting specifics: which charge appears wrong (date/amount) and what the app did incorrectly, so the disputed charge can be verified against order history per SOP-BILLING-001.

Once details are provided, verify the disputed charge against order ORD-8654 in the billing system and check refund eligibility (charge within 60 days) per SOP-BILLING-001 and POL-REFUND-001; if a duplicate or erroneous charge is confirmed, initiate a full refund (processed within 5 business days for standard tier) and inform the customer of the timeline.
Meet the support response SLA for this billing complaint: as a standard-tier customer, provide a first response within 8 business hours per POL-SLA-001.

Manually assess the ambiguous app reference ("the thing wrong") after the customer clarifies; if it turns out to be a confirmed service-impacting bug rather than a billing issue, re-route to the bug_report workflow and apply the relevant escalation rules.

classification.reason: The feedback mentions billing being 'weird' and also vaguely references the app doing 'the thing wrong,' which could indicate a bug; billing is the more concrete complaint but the text is too vague to be certain.

review_reason: classification confidence 0.40 below 0.65; report confidence 0.35 below 0.7
